summary: Matched several Kirby copied neutral-special routines in ftKb_SpecialNYs.c. The PR implemented three former decomp stubs, completed ftKb_MsSpecialNLoop_Anim, and decomp.dev reported 4 new GALE01 matches totaling +460 bytes.

key_files.role: Only changed file; implements Kirby copied-special helpers and fixes a partial animation-loop match.

what_changed: Implemented ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010A8BC. When fp->target_item_gobj is non-null, it computes a has_destroy_type flag from specific Yoshi capture motion IDs, calls it_802F2E7C on the target item, then clears fp->x1A64 and fp->target_item_gobj.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialNMs_8010B868 for the air transition path. It calls ftCommon_8007D5D4 and then Fighter_ChangeMotionState using an air motion ID selected from fp->fv.kb.hat.kind.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialNMs_8010B8E0 for the ground transition path. It calls ftCommon_8007D7FC and then Fighter_ChangeMotionState using a ground motion ID selected from fp->fv.kb.hat.kind.
Added inline helpers getAirSpecialMotionId and getGroundSpecialMotionId. Both select Ms motion IDs when fp->fv.kb.hat.kind == FTKIND_MARS and Fe motion IDs otherwise.

Adjusted ftKb_MsSpecialNLoop_Anim by changing the charge pointer from u32* to s32*, adding explicit casts to the ms/fe charge_iterations fields, and replacing ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) with ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010C148(gobj) after the charge threshold is exceeded.
smart_moves: The ground and air transition functions share the same Fighter_ChangeMotionState argument pattern while isolating only the motion-ID choice into inline helpers, which keeps the matched code simple and avoids duplicating the hat-kind branch around the full transition call.
The s32* charge pointer and explicit casts show attention to signedness as a matching constraint; this small type change helped finish ftKb_MsSpecialNLoop_Anim from a near-match.
The item cleanup helper preserves behavior order: notify the item through it_802F2E7C before clearing both related fighter item references.
The transition implementations preserve fp->cur_anim_frame and use the exact observed ChangeMotionState flags and float arguments: 0x0C4C7084, 1.0f, and 0.0f.
decomp_lessons: For Kirby copied specials, fp->fv.kb.hat.kind can drive small motion-ID selection helpers while the surrounding transition code remains identical.
Do not assume unsigned dat-attr fields are harmless in near-matches; pointer signedness and casts can affect the final generated code.

A generic animation helper call may be wrong even if it is plausible. In ftKb_MsSpecialNLoop_Anim, the matching call after the charge threshold was ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010C148, not ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining.
When implementing target-item cleanup routines, look for both an item callback and fighter-side pointer clearing in the same helper.
Bot match reports are useful evidence for validating tiny matching fixes: this PR moved ftKb_MsSpecialNLoop_Anim from 97.69% to 100% with only +3 matched bytes.
naming_conventions: Kirby fighter routines in this file use the ftKb_ prefix and often retain address-suffixed names for still-unnamed helpers, such as ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010A8BC.
Motion-state enum names use the ftKb_MS_* pattern, with variants such as ftKb_MS_MsSpecialAirNStart, ftKb_MS_FeSpecialAirNStart, ftKb_MS_MsSpecialNStart, and ftKb_MS_FeSpecialNStart.
The added local helpers use lower camelCase names: getAirSpecialMotionId and getGroundSpecialMotionId.
The file follows common fighter local naming: Fighter* fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj), ftKb_DatAttrs* da = fp->dat_attrs, and Fighter_GObj/HSD_GObj parameter types depending on the routine.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced three /// # decomp stubs with C implementations that reached 100% according to the decomp.dev report.
Used inline motion-ID selector helpers to shape the branch/result code for Marth-vs-Fe copied special state changes.
Kept exact Fighter_ChangeMotionState arguments, including flag literal 0x0C4C7084 and frame/rate/blend values fp->cur_anim_frame, 1.0f, and 0.0f.
Used s32* plus explicit casts around charge_iterations to control signed arithmetic/load behavior in ftKb_MsSpecialNLoop_Anim.
Completed the partial match by calling ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010C148 after setting fp->cmd_vars[0] = 1 instead of leaving the previous ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining call.

review_feedback.summary: GALE01 matched code increased to 55.20% (+0.01%, +460 bytes). The bot reported 4 new matches: ftKb_SpecialNMs_8010B868, ftKb_SpecialNMs_8010B8E0, ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010A8BC, and ftKb_MsSpecialNLoop_Anim.
No human review comments or reviews were present in the provided PR dump.

follow_up_queries: Search for other Kirby copied-special transition routines that branch on fp->fv.kb.hat.kind to select ftKb_MS_* motion IDs.
Audit ftKb_DatAttrs charge_iterations field signedness and other near-matches involving u32* versus s32*.
Inspect related target_item_gobj and x1A64 cleanup helpers that call it_802F2E7C.
Compare ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010C148 call sites to other special-loop animation threshold handlers.
